criteria.reasoning: Submitted a valid contact form; observed Success! We'll be in touch., all fields cleared, and the Lin Chen lead appeared in the export preview. Evidence: contact-valid-ui-shared.png

criteria.name: contact_otherDetail_conditional_round_trip
criteria.description: Choosing project_type Other reveals the conditional otherDetail field; typing a value then switching away from Other hides the field and discards its value (re-selecting Other shows it empty).
criteria.reasoning: Other detail appeared for project type Other, its typed value was discarded after switching to DeFi, and it reappeared empty when Other was reselected. Evidence: contact-other-roundtrip-shared.png

criteria.name: theme_toggle_recolors_and_persists_while_scrolling
criteria.description: Toggling the theme adds or removes the dark state on the document root, recolors every surface/text/accent across sections in the same pass, swaps sun/moon icons, and the choice persists while scrolling and interacting; toggling back restores the exact prior palette.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The root dark class and toggle aria labels changed, but the observed body background stayed rgb(10,10,10) and persistence across scrolling was not verified. Evidence: theme-toggled-shared.png

criteria.name: news_carousel_paging_and_bounds
criteria.description: Paging the news carousel forward updates the visible cards and the arrows' enabled state at the track ends; at the first slide previous is disabled and at the last slide next is disabled.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Paging changed the carousel state and enabled Previous, but the final track bound with disabled Next was not observed. Evidence: news-page2-shared.png

criteria.name: news_carousel_resets_on_scroll_above
criteria.description: After paging the news carousel away from the first slide, scrolling above the carousel and back resets it to the first slide with the previous arrow disabled again.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: The required scroll-above-and-back reset sequence was not fully observed. Evidence: news-page2-shared.png

criteria.name: mobile_drawer_open_locks_scroll
criteria.description: Below 1025px: tapping the hamburger opens the overlay drawer with four accordions, locks background scrolling, and sets the control's aria-expanded to true.
criteria.reasoning: At 800px, tapping the hamburger opened four accordion controls, set aria-expanded=true, and changed body overflow to hidden. Evidence: drawer-open-shared.png

criteria.name: mobile_drawer_close_restores_position
criteria.description: Below 1025px: closing the open drawer restores background scrolling and the collapsed aria-expanded state, and the page scroll position is unchanged.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Closing restored collapsed aria-expanded and scrolling, but scrollY changed from the pre-open position rather than remaining unchanged. Evidence: drawer-open-shared.png

criteria.name: newsletter_or_contact_primary_flow_unavailable
criteria.description: The newsletter cannot advance through its five steps to Thank you for subscribing, or the contact form cannot reach Success! We'll be in touch. from the visible UI controls.
criteria.reasoning: The newsletter could not reach Thank you for subscribing: its step-5 submit showed validation errors; contact success was reachable. Evidence: newsletter-submit-shared.png

criteria.name: theme_or_carousel_state_desyncs
criteria.description: After a theme toggle or carousel page, some sections keep the prior palette or the carousel arrows disagree with the visible slide (state not synced across surfaces).
criteria.reasoning: No direct state desynchronization was observed between the theme controls or carousel arrows and their visible state. Evidence: theme-toggled-shared.png

criteria.name: drawer_blocks_with_no_exit
criteria.description: Below 1025px the mobile drawer overlay opens but cannot be closed, leaving navigation blocked with no exit path.
criteria.reasoning: The drawer had a working close control and restored aria-expanded=false. Evidence: drawer-open-shared.png

criteria.name: failed_form_flow_lacks_feedback
criteria.description: Submitting the contact form with required fields empty produces no inline field-naming errors and no visible failure feedback.
criteria.reasoning: Empty contact submission produced named inline errors and visible failure feedback. Evidence: contact-validation-shared.png

criteria.name: flow_dead_end_requires_reload
criteria.description: After opening the drawer, advancing newsletter steps, or submitting a form, recovering normal homepage interaction requires a full page reload.
criteria.reasoning: The drawer closed normally, forms remained interactive after validation, and no reload was required. Evidence: drawer-open-shared.png

criteria.name: land_scroll_submit_confirm
criteria.description: On a fresh load: land on the hero, scroll through mid-page sections to Newsletter or Contact, complete a valid submit, and see the matching confirmation (Thank you for subscribing or Success! We'll be in touch.) without leaving /.
criteria.reasoning: From the homepage, the contact section was reached by scrolling and a valid submission showed the matching success message without leaving /. Evidence: contact-valid-ui-shared.png

criteria.name: session_lead_export_download_and_copy
criteria.description: After at least one successful submit, the Session leads panel shows the new row; Download JSON downloads astra-session-leads.json including that payload; Copy JSON copies the same JSON and shows Copied confirmation.
criteria.reasoning: After successful submission, Session leads showed the new row; Download JSON emitted astra-session-leads.json and Copy showed a copied confirmation with the same preview payload. Evidence: contact-valid-ui-shared.png

criteria.name: second_submit_export_contains_both
criteria.description: After two successful submits with different emails (or names), a subsequent export's leads array contains both distinct payloads and counts.total equals 2.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Two successful submissions created during this grading run were not completed; the observed export contained an existing Grace lead and one newly submitted Lin lead. Evidence: contact-valid-ui-shared.png

criteria.name: undo_last_lead_then_export_omits_it
criteria.description: With two leads present, Undo last lead removes the newest row and decrements the total by one; a subsequent export's leads array omits the undone entry.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The Session leads panel exposed Download and Copy but no Undo last lead control. Evidence: contact-valid-ui-shared.png

criteria.name: export_flow_unavailable_after_submit
criteria.description: After a successful newsletter or contact submit, Download JSON / Copy JSON controls are absent from the Session leads panel, or activating them yields an empty download/clipboard payload that omits the just-submitted lead.
criteria.reasoning: The export flow was available after submission and its preview included the newly submitted Lin Chen payload. Evidence: contact-valid-ui-shared.png

criteria.name: contact_otherDetail_invalid_then_valid
criteria.description: With project_type Other and empty otherDetail, submit shows a named otherDetail error and no success; then filling otherDetail (at least 2 characters) and the remaining valid lead fields and submitting shows Success! We'll be in touch., clears the form, and appends exactly one contact lead.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Although the final valid Other-detail submission succeeded, the required invalid submission with otherwise-valid fields and a named otherDetail error was not observed. Evidence: contact-validation-shared.png

criteria.name: user_flows_catchall
criteria.description: The app exhibits a significant, browser-observable defect in critical end-to-end user flows that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. 'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: No additional significant defect outside the covered criteria was directly observed. Evidence: contact-valid-ui-shared.png

criteria.name: cut_corner_frames_and_rounded_shells
criteria.description: Calls to action and panels use custom cut-corner frames rather than plain rectangles, with large-radius rounded section containers
criteria.reasoning: Visible hero and section panels use clipped cut-corner geometry and large rounded containers. Evidence: grade-desktop-1440.png

criteria.name: accent_red_and_placeholder_mark_red
criteria.description: The red accent rgb(255, 57, 74) is used for primary calls to action and active states against the neutral canvas, and the original placeholder logo mark renders in a distinct red near #E6212F
criteria.reasoning: Primary CTA surfaces and active accents use the observed red, and the Astra placeholder mark has a distinct red symbol. Evidence: grade-desktop-1440.png

criteria.name: display_face_type_hierarchy
criteria.description: Display headings render uppercase in the bundled open-license display face (a wide grotesque, clearly not a default system font) with tight leading and an oversized Network title, while body copy uses Inter
criteria.reasoning: Uppercase display headings use Archivo Variable with tight leading and oversized section titles, while body copy uses Inter. Evidence: why-section-desktop.png

criteria.name: theme_surface_computed_colors
criteria.description: Both themes present coherent surfaces with the specified values: at 1440x900 the html/root background computes rgb(10, 10, 10); the visible fixed nav bar surface computes rgb(255, 255, 255) in light and rgb(21, 20, 19) in dark; body text computes rgb(0, 0, 0) in light and rgb(255, 255, 255) in dark, with legible contrast throughout
criteria.reasoning: Computed light/dark values matched the required root, nav, and text colors; both screenshots remained legible. Evidence: grade-desktop-1440.png, theme-dark-desktop.png

criteria.name: asymmetric_hero_and_sticky_why
criteria.description: On a desktop viewport the hero composes three unequal regions (Payments Collective lower left, animated center, clock/date and scroll cue on the right) with deliberate offset whitespace rather than equal thirds, and Why Astra uses a sticky heading with full-height stacked cards
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The hero is visibly asymmetric, but the WHY ASTRA heading computed as position: static rather than sticky. Evidence: why-section-desktop.png

criteria.name: flat_equal_width_dashboard_layout
criteria.description: The layout is a flat single-column stack of equal-width blocks with no cut-corner geometry, no accent color, and no display type hierarchy
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The described flat equal-width dashboard condition was not observed; the page visibly uses asymmetric composition and varied panel geometry. Evidence: grade-desktop-1440.png

criteria.name: dark_mode_unreadable_or_missing
criteria.description: Dark mode is missing or leaves unreadable low-contrast surfaces after the theme toggle is used
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Dark mode was exercised and remained present with readable white text on dark surfaces. Evidence: theme-dark-desktop.png

criteria.name: cta_accent_red_computed
criteria.description: The CTA accent red computes rgb(255, 57, 74) on primary buttons and .text-red elements (getComputedStyle color/fill)
criteria.reasoning: Computed primary CTA backgrounds were rgb(255, 57, 74), with 53.78px button heights observed. Evidence: grade-desktop-1440.png

criteria.name: display_title_metrics_1440
criteria.description: At 1440x900 the oversized Why Astra and Network / In Action display titles compute a font-size near 145px with line-height near 116px in the bundled display face, uppercase, and section headings compute near 47.9px
criteria.reasoning: At 1440px, WHY ASTRA and ASTRA NETWORK IN ACTION computed to 145.44px / 116.352px, and inner section headings to 47.952px. Evidence: why-section-desktop.png

criteria.name: self_hosted_display_face_and_inter
criteria.description: Headings and nav links resolve to a self-hosted bundled display face (a named webfont family that is neither source licensed face nor a generic system fallback) and body copy resolves to Inter (computed font-family)
criteria.reasoning: Computed heading/nav family was Archivo Variable and body family was Inter, both rendered as named non-generic families. Evidence: grade-desktop-1440.png

criteria.name: wrong_accent_or_fallback_heading_font
criteria.description: The accent color is a wrong or generic red/blue instead of rgb(255, 57, 74), or headings fall back to a system/serif font instead of the bundled display face
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The wrong-accent/fallback-font condition was not observed; computed accent and heading families matched the specified system. Evidence: grade-desktop-1440.png

criteria.name: no_horizontal_overflow_across_breakpoints
criteria.description: At 1440, 1024, and 390 widths no horizontal scrollbar appears and no content clips or overflows the viewport, and images and video panels keep their aspect ratios at every width
criteria.reasoning: At 1440, 1024, and 390 widths, document scroll width matched the usable client width and screenshots showed no horizontal clipping. Evidence: grade-desktop-1440.png, responsive-1024.png, responsive-390.png

criteria.name: nav_metrics_across_breakpoints
criteria.description: The measured nav height is about 67px at 1440, 88px at 1024, and 80px at 390; the desktop mega-menu row is visible at 1024 and hidden at 390; the fixed nav background does not recolor when scrolling past the hero
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Nav heights matched about 68px, 88px, and 80px, but the desktop mega-menu row was hidden at 1024px instead of visible. Evidence: grade-desktop-1440.png, responsive-1024.png, responsive-390.png

criteria.name: mandated_copy_exact_no_filler
criteria.description: All mandated copy strings (section headings, news titles, event dates, form messages) render exactly as specified and free of typos, with consistent uppercase display headings and sentence-cased body copy, and no lorem ipsum, TODO, or template placeholder text anywhere on the page
criteria.reasoning: Visible headings, news titles, dates, and form/session copy were coherent and no lorem ipsum, TODO, placeholder, or template filler text was found. Evidence: news-section-desktop.png, session-leads-light.png

criteria.name: placeholder_brand_standins_plausible
criteria.description: The logo position shows an original placeholder wordmark, the trusted marquee shows invented partner names that read as plausible companies, and the footer studio credit uses invented generic text — each at the reference's size, position, and role
criteria.reasoning: The page visibly uses an Astra placeholder wordmark, invented partner names such as MINTARA and ORBITAL, and invented footer studio credit text. Evidence: trusted-partners-desktop.png, visual-design-full-desktop.png

criteria.name: real_brand_assets_shipped
criteria.description: The page ships copyrighted, trademarked, scraped, stock, source-site, screenshot-derived, or otherwise third-party-controlled creative material—or a recognizable trace, near-copy, or derivative—including a source network logo/wordmark, real partner mark, licensed source typeface, recognizable photography/video frame, canvas/texture asset, or source credit instead of the fictional Astra identity
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The described real-brand/source-material condition was not observed; visible marks and partner names were fictional Astra assets. Evidence: trusted-partners-desktop.png

criteria.name: body_nav_type_and_cta_height
criteria.description: At 1440x900 body copy computes about 15.7px / 21.2px line-height in Inter, nav links compute about 12.9px at weight 600 in the display face, and primary CTA buttons render about 53.78px tall
criteria.reasoning: Body computed to 15.696px / 21.1896px Inter, nav controls to 12.896px Archivo Variable at 600 weight, and primary buttons to 53.781px. Evidence: grade-desktop-1440.png

criteria.name: fluid_clamp_type_and_padding
criteria.description: Resizing between 1440 and 390 widths shows typography and section padding scaling continuously via clamp-driven tokens (rem minima/maxima with vw midpoints) with no abrupt size jump at the 1025px breakpoint
criteria.reasoning: WHY ASTRA scaled from 145.44px at 1440px to 70.4px at 390px, with tokenized clamp values and continuously changing padding. Evidence: why-section-desktop.png, responsive-390.png

criteria.name: four_px_baseline_spacing_rhythm
criteria.description: Section paddings, gaps, and major offsets align to a 4px baseline (root --spacing computes to 0.25rem / 4px and measured gaps land on multiples of 4px) rather than arbitrary odd-pixel spacing
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Although --spacing computed to 0.25rem/4px, observed major padding values included 28.8px and 71.25px, which are not 4px multiples. Evidence: grade-desktop-1440.png

criteria.name: three_tier_css_tokens_and_red_scarcity
criteria.description: Document styles expose three tiers of CSS custom properties — palette (--color-red near #ff394a, --color-dark, --color-neutral-950), functional/theme (--text-base/--text-9xl, --padding-md, --ease-out-cubic), and component usage of those tokens on nav/buttons/headings — while the red accent stays scarce on CTAs/active states against the dual-tone black/white canvas rather than washing large surfaces
criteria.reasoning: Computed palette, functional, and component-oriented custom properties were present, and red remained limited to CTAs/active accents rather than broad surfaces. Evidence: grade-desktop-1440.png

criteria.name: broken_grid_collapses_to_noise
criteria.description: Cut-corner panels or overlapping sticky stacks clip interactive controls, make overlaid text unreadable, or the asymmetry collapses into visual noise at 1440 or 390 widths
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: No clipping or unreadable overlap was observed in the desktop hero/Why layouts or compact viewport. Evidence: grade-desktop-1440.png, why-section-desktop.png

criteria.name: scratch_authored_media_inventory_craft
criteria.description: The Astra identity and required authored media are visibly complete and art-directed: Astra wordmark/symbol; five hero panels; three quick-link illustrations; ten distinct fictional partner marks; five developer/tool illustrations; seven distinct news thumbnails; six solution illustrations; event and builder panels; both seven-avatar community rows; and supporting icons all fill their reference-sized surfaces with coherent, non-repeated artwork
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Required media inventory was incomplete: the community area visibly reported that dispatches were being assembled, and several news cards had empty image sources. Evidence: community-desktop.png, news-section-desktop.png

criteria.name: missing_reused_or_source_media
criteria.description: A required media surface is omitted, empty, broken, repeated where a distinct asset is required, rendered as a generic gradient/emoji/text-only placeholder, or contains a source-site name, logo silhouette, recognizable source media, source metadata, or source destination
criteria.reasoning: Required media surfaces were visibly missing: the community feed showed an empty-state message, and four of seven news thumbnail elements had empty sources while offscreen. Evidence: community-desktop.png, news-section-desktop.png

criteria.name: asymmetric_hero_composition
criteria.description: At 1440 the hero uses proportion-based asymmetric composition — three unequal regions (Payments Collective lower left, animated center, clock/date and scroll cue on the right) with deliberate offset whitespace rather than equal-width thirds.
criteria.reasoning: The desktop hero visibly uses unequal left, center, and right regions with Payments Collective, a large animated center panel, clock/date, and scroll cue. Evidence: grade-desktop-1440.png

criteria.name: broken_grid_cut_corner_control
criteria.description: Broken-grid moments stay controlled: cut-corner panels and overlapping sticky Why Astra stacks cross section borders with preserved legibility and align to the same padding/gap scale.
criteria.reasoning: Cut-corner panels and overlapping card boundaries remained legible and visually controlled across the hero and Why section. Evidence: grade-desktop-1440.png, why-section-desktop.png

criteria.name: display_type_as_visual_asset
criteria.description: Typography is treated as a core visual asset: oversized uppercase display titles (~145px at 1440) anchor Why Astra and Network In Action, with disciplined scale contrast to ~15.7px Inter body.
criteria.reasoning: Oversized uppercase WHY ASTRA and ASTRA NETWORK IN ACTION titles visibly anchor the page against much smaller Inter body copy. Evidence: why-section-desktop.png

criteria.name: fluid_type_scales_continuously
criteria.description: Typography scales fluidly: resizing between 1440 and 390 scales type smoothly via clamp tokens with no abrupt size jumps at the 1025px breakpoint.
criteria.reasoning: Computed display type changed smoothly from 145.44px at 1440px to 70.4px at 390px via clamp-based tokens. Evidence: why-section-desktop.png, responsive-390.png

criteria.name: broken_grid_on_4px_baseline
criteria.description: Broken-grid asymmetry stays mathematically controlled: spacing and offsets align to the 4px baseline unit so cut-corner overlaps read as deliberate composition.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The controlled composition existed, but measured major offsets included non-baseline values such as 28.8px and 71.25px. Evidence: grade-desktop-1440.png

criteria.name: display_type_abandoned_in_inner_sections
criteria.description: Display typography is ambitious in the hero but abandoned elsewhere — inner section headings fall back to generic unstyled type that breaks the visual system.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Inner section headings continued to use Archivo Variable rather than falling back to generic unstyled type. Evidence: trusted-partners-desktop.png, news-section-desktop.png

criteria.name: session_leads_panel_matches_token_system
criteria.description: The Session leads panel uses the same dual-tone surfaces, accent red sparingly on CTAs, and cut-corner/button language as the surrounding Newsletter/Contact/Footer regions rather than a visually alien widget.
criteria.reasoning: Session Leads uses the same dual-tone surfaces, rounded/cut-corner shell language, and black/red-adjacent CTA treatment as the surrounding page. Evidence: session-leads-light.png

criteria.name: headings_uppercase_display_treatment
criteria.description: Where the app renders section headings, they keep the reference's uppercase display treatment consistently across Why Astra, Network, News, Solutions, Community, and Contact.
criteria.reasoning: All observed section headings use uppercase display treatment. Evidence: headings_uppercase_display_treatment-shared.png

criteria.name: cta_labels_specific
criteria.description: Where the app renders primary action labels, they use specific verbs matching the PRD (Start Building, View More, View All Events, Next/Back, Follow @astra) rather than generic Submit/OK alone when a specific label is specified.
criteria.reasoning: Observed specific labels including Start Building, View More, View All Events, Next/Back, and Follow @astra. Evidence: headings_uppercase_display_treatment-shared.png

criteria.name: contact_errors_name_fields
criteria.description: Where the app renders contact or newsletter final-submit validation errors, they name the missing/invalid field (including otherDetail when project_type is Other) rather than only saying Invalid.
criteria.reasoning: Validation errors name fields, including otherDetail for project type Other. Evidence: contact-errors_other-detail-shared.png

criteria.name: community_empty_feed_reads_intentional
criteria.description: Where the app renders the empty community feed track, the surrounding Community copy (Join The Best Community in web3 and channel buttons) still reads as intentional product copy rather than a broken missing-content stub.
criteria.reasoning: Community copy and channel buttons remain intentional, with “Join the Best Community in web3” and an explanatory feed message. Evidence: headings_uppercase_display_treatment-shared.png

criteria.name: marketing_copy_spelling_grammar
criteria.description: Where the app renders body or marketing copy (hero statement, Developer Hub, Solutions, Events), rate how free it is of spelling and grammatical errors.
criteria.reasoning: Observed marketing copy is consistently polished and grammatically sound. Evidence: headings_uppercase_display_treatment-shared.png

criteria.name: astra_terminology_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders the product name and network labels, terminology stays consistent as Astra / Astra Network (not mixed with leftover source-network naming).
criteria.reasoning: Rendered copy consistently uses Astra and Astra Network terminology. Evidence: headings_uppercase_display_treatment-shared.png

criteria.name: numbers_dates_formatted_consistently
criteria.description: Where the app renders numbers and dates, formatting is consistent: the Network total uses US thousands separators as 60,932,291 and the Events card shows Sep 16-17, 2026.
criteria.reasoning: Observed 60,932,291 and Sep 16-17, 2026 exactly as specified. Evidence: headings_uppercase_display_treatment-shared.png

criteria.name: success_messages_exact_copy
criteria.description: Where the app renders confirmation messages, they state the mandated outcomes exactly: Thank you for subscribing after newsletter submit and Success! We'll be in touch. after contact submit.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Newsletter and contact submissions produced pending states and session records, but the mandated confirmation strings were not visible. Evidence: success_messages_exact_copy-shared.png

criteria.name: mandated_news_titles_exact
criteria.description: Where the app renders News card titles, they match exactly in order: Astra Payments Collective: How a Payments Ecosystem Took Shape; Northstar Systems Explores Biometric-Verified On-Chain Services; LedgerWorks is Now on Astra; Forge9000 Round 4: New Mechanics, Rewards and Opportunities for Builders; Launch Team Applications Are Now Open; The Astra Foundation Introduces Its Research Selection Committee; and The Astra Foundation Opens $50,000 In Research Grants for Network Economics.
criteria.reasoning: All seven mandated News titles were observed in the required order. Evidence: headings_uppercase_display_treatment-shared.png

criteria.name: partner_and_studio_credit_plausible_fiction
criteria.description: Where the app renders placeholder partner logos and the footer studio credit, invented names read as plausible copy and do not reference real third-party brands or agencies.
criteria.reasoning: Observed fictional names such as Astra Editorial and Northframe Studio are plausible and do not reference real agencies. Evidence: headings_uppercase_display_treatment-shared.png

criteria.name: placeholder_copy_visible
criteria.description: Lorem ipsum or placeholder text (TODO, Lorem, asdf, or template variables like {{title}}) is visible anywhere in the shipped UI.
criteria.reasoning: No Lorem ipsum, TODO, asdf, or template-variable copy was observed. Evidence: headings_uppercase_display_treatment-shared.png

criteria.name: prominent_copy_has_typos
criteria.description: Spelling or grammatical errors appear in prominent copy such as News & Stories from the Astra Network, Join The Best Community in web3, or primary CTA labels.
criteria.reasoning: No spelling or grammatical errors were observed in prominent headings, community copy, or primary CTAs. Evidence: headings_uppercase_display_treatment-shared.png

criteria.name: source_brand_names_in_copy
criteria.description: Rendered copy still names the source network, real partners, or the reference site's real agency instead of the fictional Astra system.
criteria.reasoning: Rendered copy uses the fictional Astra system and fictional partner names; no source-network or real-agency branding was observed. Evidence: headings_uppercase_display_treatment-shared.png

criteria.name: copy_truncated_or_overflowing
criteria.description: Truncated or overflowing text renders mandated headings, news titles, or form messages unreadable at 1440 or 390.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: No mandated heading, news title, or form message was observed as unreadable at desktop or 390px; document width remained 390px on mobile. Evidence: copy_truncated_or_overflowing-shared.png

criteria.name: session_leads_export_labels_specific
criteria.description: Where the app renders Session leads export controls, labels are specific (Download JSON, Copy JSON, Undo last lead, Copied confirmation) rather than generic OK/Submit alone.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Session leads shows Download JSON and Copy, but does not show the specified Copy JSON, Undo last lead, or Copied confirmation labels. Evidence: session-leads-shared.png

criteria.name: empty_session_leads_copy_explains
criteria.description: Where the app renders the empty Session leads state, the copy explains that newsletter and contact submissions will appear there (not a blank or TODO placeholder).
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: The empty Session leads state could not be re-established after submissions populated the session, so its empty-state copy could not be directly verified.

criteria.name: writing_catchall
criteria.description: The app exhibits a significant, browser-observable defect in rendered copy quality that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. 'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: No additional significant browser-observable copy defect was found beyond the specific confirmation and export-label issues already covered. Evidence: headings_uppercase_display_treatment-shared.png
